title: Fillman Platform Twin Bed
summary: A plain 2x4 box on short 2x3 legs with a raised 1x3 lip that gives it a platform-bed look, plus cleats supporting slats instead of a box spring.
description:
Start with a simple 2x4 box, checked for square by comparing the two diagonals corner to corner before anything else gets attached. Short 2x3 legs go on next — 2x4s work fine here too if that's what's on hand, and this is also the point to taper the legs or swap in decorative feet if you want a different look than the plain square leg shown.

**The 2x2 cleats are what the slats actually rest on** — attach them with predrilled holes and 2-1/2" screws, level along the inside of the box, before anything else goes on top. The 1x3 lip pieces come next, glued and screwed down around the top edge of the box, which is what gives this bed its raised "platform" look rather than reading as a plain box frame.

A 1x3 footboard piece screws and glues to the bottom of the bed, then the slats go down last — spanning between the cleats. **Depending on mattress quality, fewer slats than shown may be enough**, but don't cut below what your mattress manufacturer recommends for support.

materials.name: 1x3 or 1x2 slats, 8 ft (or scraps of matching thickness)
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.material: 1x3 or 1x2 slats, 8 ft (or scraps of matching thickness)

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Build a plain 2x4 box and check it's square by measuring the two diagonals from opposite corners.

steps.title: Add the legs
steps.body: Attach the four legs to the box. Taper them or swap in decorative feet if you want a different look.

steps.title: Attach the slat cleats
steps.body: Predrill and attach the two slat cleats level along the inside of the box with 2-1/2" screws.

steps.title: Add the platform lip
steps.body: Glue and screw the side and footboard lip pieces around the top edge of the box.

steps.title: Add the slats
steps.body: Lay the slats across the cleats, spaced evenly — fewer slats may work depending on mattress quality, but don't go below the mattress manufacturer's minimum.
materials: 1x3 or 1x2 slats, 8 ft (or scraps of matching thickness)

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly
